title: Runtime and cost
summary: Keep model behavior inside latency, retry, and spend budgets without hiding degraded quality.
decision: How much model work, retrieval depth, and response complexity are acceptable for the workflow.

intent.purpose: Balance user experience, reliability, and spend by making runtime limits explicit.
intent.failure_mode: The system loops, overthinks, or burns spend to produce marginally better output while the user waits.
intent.good_judgment: Use the simplest path that still meets quality needs, and surface when quality is intentionally constrained by budget or latency.
acceptable_variation: reduced retrieval depth for low-risk questions
higher spend only for explicitly high-value paths
non_negotiables: no hidden fallback that fabricates certainty
no premium-cost path without a documented reason

detection.decision_question: Is the workflow staying inside documented latency and spend budgets without creating silent quality regressions?

thresholds.pass: within target latency and token budget

low.description: Shorten the path and log a budget pressure event.

medium.description: Use the cheaper path and expose that the answer may be incomplete.

high.description: Stop the response and route to an asynchronous or human-assisted path.
